cit.basis: Flat 9% corporate income tax (társasági adó, Act LXXXI of 1996) on taxable profit — the lowest headline CIT rate in the EU.
cit.note: Administered by the National Tax and Customs Administration (NAV). A minimum-tax (income floor) rule may apply if declared profit is below 2% of adjusted revenue.

local_business_tax.name: HIPA (helyi iparűzési adó)
local_business_tax.note: Municipal local business tax of up to 2% levied on adjusted net turnover (revenue less COGS, subcontractors, materials, mediated services and direct R&D) — a different base than CIT profit; rate set per municipality. Not computed by this estimator.

kiva.note: KIVA small-business tax: optional 10% alternative regime replacing CIT and the social contribution tax; base is broadly payroll plus capital movements. Eligibility caps on headcount and revenue apply. Not computed by this estimator.

sector_surtaxes.note: Sector-specific surtaxes apply on top of CIT for banks and financial institutions, retail, energy, insurance and telecom, mostly turnover-based with progressive scales. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

pillar_two.note: Hungary implements the EU Pillar Two directive including a QDMTT (Act LXXXIV of 2023): in-scope groups (revenue >= EUR 750m) are topped up to a 15% effective rate despite the 9% headline CIT, with HIPA and certain other taxes counting toward the effective-rate computation.

vat.note: ÁFA (general turnover tax): 27% standard rate — the highest in the EU; 18% and 5% reduced rates for specified goods and services. EU One-Stop-Shop applies to cross-border B2C supplies.

personal_income_tax.note: Flat 15% personal income tax (SZJA); family and under-25/under-30 base allowances can reduce the effective burden.

social_insurance.note: 18.5% employee social security contribution (TB-járulék) and 13% employer social contribution tax (szociális hozzájárulási adó).

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
HIPA (municipal, turnover-based), KIVA, sector surtaxes, Pillar Two top-up, minimum-tax floor, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
